verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 encodes a large (~2700 aa) tetrameric Ca²⁺-release channel residing in the endoplasmic reticulum membrane, where it mediates IP3-triggered Ca²⁺ release from ER stores. Its transmembrane domains span the ER membrane (not the plasma membrane), with functional domains facing the ER lumen and cytosol — none face the extracellular space. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs (antibody, ADC, CAR-T) were identified. Pre-`no` checklist: (1) no gene-specific evidence of cell-death- or oncogenic-state outer-leaflet display — cancer involvement is strictly intracellular ER-mitochondria Ca²⁺ signaling; (2) no tissue/germline-restricted PM pool documented; (3) not a lysosomal TM protein — ER-resident; (4) documented mislocalization in disease is to nucleus or MAM, not PM; (5) no covalent or wash-resistant tethering to any PM-anchored partner. Verdict: endomembrane-resident ER channel with no route to the outer leaflet.
